name: Bhai Gurdiyal Singh Rasiya (UK)
body: Bhai Gurdiyal Singh Ji Rasiya began his initial training with his father, Dr. Sewa Singh Ji. He also went on to learn from various other renowned Raagis such as Bhai Santa Singh Kanwal and Bhai Mangal Singh from Delhi. He also picked up knowledge from a few other Raagis as well. Bhai Gurdiyal Singh Ji has been performing Kirtan since the 1960s and is adept at singing in various Raags and Taals. He has performed in many Raag Darbars in the United Kingdon (UK) and also other parts of the world. For the past 45 years or so, he has been living in the UK. A special thanks to Veer Harkirat Singh Ji for providing us with the above information. If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Gurdiyal Singh Rasiya to share, please contact us.

The next few recordings were recorded in Leeds and contributed by Dr. Manjit Singh Hunjin, who is a very talented artist. Please check out his Instagram page at the following link https://www.instagram.com/amsimeetart/?hl=en

The following recordings were contributed by Amarjit Singh Jagdev whose late father, Sardar Tarlochan Singh Jagdev made these recordings. Veer Amarjit Singh Ji has his own Youtube channel called ‘Eternal Shabad Gurbani’. Please check out and subscribe to his channel at the following link https://www.youtube.com/user/asjagdev

The following recordings are from a Kirtan program at Havelock Road Gurdwara Sahib (UK) in 1998. These were uploaded onto the Kirtan Legends Youtube channel. Please click on the following link to listen to more Kirtan on their channel https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCKkzWMUmiw2sj6QikJrjtVQ

The next few recordings were contributed by Sahib Singh Bakshi, who has his own Youtube channel. Please check out his Youtube channel for more wonderful gems at the following link https://www.youtube.com/user/sahibsingh100

The next two recordings were contributed by Pertahp Singh Tuli from Toronto. He is also running the SikhVibes Soundcloud page. Please check out his Soundcloud page for more gems at the following page https://soundcloud.com/sikhvibes
The recordings below were digitized from a spool and kindly provided to The Sikh Education Service by the Bhamra Family, Coventry.
